sourceMovie.title: Maria
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Maria works as a history movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Selena
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maria clicked for you, Selena (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Maria?
faq.answer: Try Selena, Cadillac Records, Rocketman—each shares DNA with Maria while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Maria?
faq.answer: Maria (2024) is best described as History, Drama, Music. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Maria worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
